title: Chicken Enchiladas

overview.description: Soft corn tortillas filled with seasoned chicken, rolled and baked in a rich red chilli and tomato sauce with melted cheese.

ingredients: 1–2 dried ancho or other mild dried chillies, deseeded
method: Place the chicken thighs in a saucepan with the quartered onion, garlic, bay leaf and a generous pinch of salt.
Cover with water and bring to a gentle simmer.
Cook for 20–25 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
Remove the chicken and reserve the cooking liquid.
Allow the chicken to cool slightly, then shred it with two forks.
Heat the oil in a saucepan over a medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until softened.
Add the garlic, cumin, oregano and ground coriander.
Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the dried chillies, chopped tomatoes and chicken stock.
Simmer for 15–20 minutes until the sauce has thickened and the chillies have softened.
Blend the sauce until smooth, adding a little reserved chicken cooking liquid if necessary.
Taste and adjust the seasoning.
Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan).
Mix enough of the enchilada sauce into the shredded chicken to keep the filling moist.
Warm the tortillas briefly so they become flexible.
Spread a little sauce over the base of a baking dish.
Fill each tortilla with some of the chicken and roll tightly.
Arrange the enchiladas seam-side down in the baking dish.
Pour over the remaining sauce.
Scatter with the grated cheese.
Bake for 20–25 minutes until hot and bubbling.
Finish with fresh coriander and serve with sour cream and lime wedges.

learning.did-you-know: The word enchilada comes from the Spanish enchilar, meaning 'to season with chilli'. The defining feature is the tortilla being covered in chilli sauce rather than simply being used as a wrap.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to poach and shred chicken, make a chilli-based sauce and assemble filled tortillas for baking.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A comforting Mexican dish that builds on the family-friendly appeal of fajitas while introducing a different way to use tortillas, chilli sauces and shredded chicken.
chapman-tips: Keep the chicken moist before filling the tortillas so the finished enchiladas do not become dry.
Warm the tortillas before rolling them. Cold corn tortillas are much more likely to crack.
Don't drown the tortillas in sauce before rolling or they can become difficult to handle.
The sauce should be flavourful enough to stand on its own because it is a major part of the finished dish.
A little cheese is enough; the dish should not become dominated by melted cheese.
If using dried chillies, remove the seeds and membranes if you want a milder sauce.
notes.batch-cooking: Assemble the enchiladas in a freezer-safe dish, cover tightly and freeze before baking. Thaw in the refrigerator before baking, or bake from frozen at a lower temperature until heated through before uncovering and browning the cheese.
notes.leftovers: Leftover enchiladas reheat well in the oven or microwave. Shredded leftover chicken and sauce can also be used in quesadillas or burritos.
